name: Garuda

visual_attributes.morphology: Eagle-like form with partially open wings, sometimes anthropomorphic with human features
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Massive, capable of blocking out the sun
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Wings slightly open, either in zoomorphic or anthropomorphic form

domain_specific_visual.ICONOGRAPHY: Kneeling figure with serpents, hands in namaste posture, sometimes carrying Vishnu

domain_specific_functional.SYMBOLISM: Iconic symbol of kings' duty and power, insignia of royalty or dharma

relationships.description: Garuda is the son of the sage Kashyapa.
Garuda is the son of Vinata.
Garuda is the younger brother of Aruna, the charioteer of the Sun.
Garuda serves as the mount (vahana) of the Hindu god Vishnu.
Garuda is an enemy of every serpent.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of power, courage, and protection; associated with royalty and dharma

contrastive_constraints: NOT a simple bird without anthropomorphic features
